seo.title: Building Workflow Automation with Instructor + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build workflow automation with Instructor + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Instructor simplifies structured output extraction from LLMs by patching clients to return validated Pydantic models, perfect for workflow automation agents that need reliable task planning and execution. In multi-step workflows with API calls, conditional logic, and human approvals, Instructor's parsing can fail due to hallucinated enums, missing fields, or inconsistent nesting. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify action correctness, permissions, idempotency, and rollback safety, ensuring production reliability.

architectureOverview.heading: Workflow Automation Architecture with Instructor
architectureOverview.description:
A typical Instructor-powered workflow automation agent follows a multi-step execution pattern: the LLM generates a structured TaskPlan (Pydantic model) containing sequenced actions, conditions, and API payloads. The agent executes actions sequentially, calling external APIs while validating permissions and handling human-in-the-loop approvals via webhooks or queues. Conditional branching and error recovery use the structured plan's metadata for decision-making.

Instructor patches the OpenAI client to enforce schema validation on TaskPlan extraction, retrying on failures like missing required fields. Vex wraps these extractions and action executions to verify business logic invariants. Rollback mechanisms track state changes across steps, enabling safe recovery from partial failures.

The architecture scales via async task queues (Celery/ BullMQ) and integrates with approval systems like Slack or custom UIs, with all critical decisions flowing through verified Instructor outputs.

risks.name: Structured output validation retries exhausted
risks.description: Instructor retries fail when LLM consistently produces invalid TaskPlan structures
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies final output meets workflow invariants before execution

risks.name: Partial extraction missing required fields
risks.description: TaskPlan missing critical fields like 'api_endpoint' or 'rollback_steps'
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces complete field presence and semantic validity

risks.name: Model hallucinating enum values outside schema
risks.description: LLM generates invalid ActionType enum values like 'CREATE_USER_ACCOUNT'
risks.mitigation: Vex validates enum values against business-allowed operations

risks.name: Nested object extraction producing inconsistent depth
risks.description: Inconsistent nesting in conditional_branches or rollback_steps arrays
risks.mitigation: Vex checks structural consistency across workflow steps

implementation.title: Instructor TaskPlan Pydantic Model
implementation.description: Core structured output model for workflow steps with actions and conditions
implementation.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import List, Literal, Optional
from instructor import from_openai

class Action(BaseModel):
    type: Literal['api_call', 'human_approval', 'condition_check']
    endpoint: Optional[str] = None
    payload: dict = {}
    idempotency_key: str

class TaskPlan(BaseModel):
    steps: List[Action] = Field(..., min_items=1, max_items=10)
    rollback_steps: List[Action] = []
    requires_approval: bool = False

client = from_openai(OpenAI())

task_plan = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o",
    response_model=TaskPlan,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Plan workflow to update user profile"}]
)

vexIntegration.title: Verify TaskPlan Action Correctness
vexIntegration.description: Ensure generated actions match allowed operations and have valid payloads
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import verify

@verify(guards=[
    "action.type in ['api_call', 'human_approval']",
    "action.endpoint in allowed_endpoints",
    "len(action.payload) > 0 if action.type == 'api_call' else True"
])
def safe_task_plan(plan: TaskPlan) -> TaskPlan:
    return plan

# Usage
verified_plan = safe_task_plan(task_plan)
executor.execute(verified_plan)
